title: Pyxis of al-Mughira
topic_path: Art of the Islamic world 640 to now

assessment_items.question_content:
**A pyxis is a cylindrical box used for . . .**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/26422fa04107194f8c4a97b2d261765f.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
** The Pyxis of al-Mughira held silver containers of perfume.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-pyxis-of-al-mughira)**

assessment_items.question_content: **The Pyxis of al-Mughira is among the best surviving examples of royal [[☃ dropdown 1]] carving in [[☃ dropdown 2]].**
hints:
**The Pyxis of al-Mughira is among the best surviving examples of the royal ivory carving tradition in Al-Andalus.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-pyxis-of-al-mughira)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**This medallion shows two men collecting eggs from the nests of falcons, which were a symbol of Umayyad . . .**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/74ce864fe22db034bbe31c2d52746aa9.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The falcon imagery may come from contemporary poetry and suggests legitimacy or power.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-pyxis-of-al-mughira)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**This medallion shows a lion attacking a bull. In Arabic poetry, these lions symbolize . . .**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**These lions symbolize the victorious (in this case, perhaps the Umayyads).**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-1300-medieval---byzantine-eras/islamic-art/a/medieval-period-pyxis-of-al-mughira)**
